title: Austroplenckia populnea
intro: Austroplenckia populnea, also known as the desert raisin, is a drought-resistant, deciduous shrub native to Australia. The health benefits of Austroplenckia populnea include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Austroplenckia populnea is often used to make teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments.

Within the Sapindaceae family, Austroplenckia populnea is part of the tribe Cardioseae, characterized by its unique flower morphology.
